( NCT03703882 ). Vamorolone is a glucocorticoid that stabilizes membranes by reducing lipid peroxidation and inhibiting NF-kB-mediated inflammation; it has also demonstrated functional improvement of symptoms. Recombinant Adeno-associated viral (rAAV): stimulates overexpression of MBNL1, to prevent sequestration. Inhibition of CUG-BP1 activity via small molecules (pentamidine) or by inhibiting protein kinase C (involved in activating CUG-BP1) can also prevent sequestration. Clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeats (CRISPR/Cas): cleave and degrade CUG mRNA expansion. Document [15] (Title: Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy -- Treatment / Management -- Cardiomyopathy) Surveillance consists of a cardiology assessment with ECG and echocardiogram. Document [16] (Title: Becker Muscular Dystrophy -- Treatment / Management -- Pharmacological Therapy of Muscle Dystrophy) Corticosteroids should be started before physical disability develops and must continue even after losing ambulation. Glucocorticoid treatment helps prolong survival by delaying the onset of cardiomyopathy, improving pulmonary function, retarding scoliosis, and reducing the need for spinal surgery. [28] [29] The steroid dose should be reduced by 25% to 33% if adverse effects develop. Nitric oxide is occasionally prescribed to improve muscular circulation.

Document [20] (Title: Muscular Dystrophy -- Treatment / Management -- Other Interventions) Genetic counseling is recommended, especially for X-linked inheritance cases. Genetic counseling should be extended to the mother, female siblings, offspring, and maternal relatives.

Document [31] (Title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2080) Cidofovir is classified as a member of the pharmacologic group “nucleosides and nucleotides excluding reverse transcriptase inhibitors,” indicating that it belongs to the broader category of nucleoside and nucleotide analogues but does not function as a reverse transcriptase inhibitor. This classification helps differentiate cidofovir’s mechanism of action and therapeutic use from other antiviral agents that target reverse transcriptase.
